name: Tesa/IPA Protocol
subtitle: Tesamorelin + Ipamorelin GH Secretagogue Blend
overview: The Tesa/IPA blend combines two complementary growth hormone secretagogues: Tesamorelin (a GHRH analog that stimulates GH release from the pituitary) and Ipamorelin (a selective ghrelin mimetic/GHRP that amplifies GH pulses). This combination addresses both the GHRH and GHRP pathways for synergistic GH release. Common formulations include 5mg/5mg (1:1 ratio) and 10mg/3mg (higher Tesamorelin) variants. Tesamorelin is FDA-approved for HIV-associated lipodystrophy, while Ipamorelin remains investigational.
mechanism: The blend leverages two distinct GH-releasing pathways: Tesamorelin is a synthetic GHRH analog that directly stimulates growth hormone-releasing hormone receptors on pituitary somatotrophs, triggering GH synthesis and secretion in a pulsatile, physiological manner. Ipamorelin is a selective ghrelin receptor (GHS-R1a) agonist that amplifies GH pulses without significantly affecting cortisol or prolactin. Together, they produce synergistic GH release greater than either compound alone, while maintaining the body's natural feedback mechanisms.
keyBenefits: Synergistic GH release from dual pathways
Does not significantly affect cortisol or prolactin
May improve body composition, sleep, and recovery

items.name: Fat Reduction
items.description: Tesamorelin has demonstrated fat reduction in clinical trials; synergy with Ipamorelin may enhance effects.

items.name: Visceral Fat Loss
items.description: Tesamorelin specifically reduces visceral adipose tissue (FDA-approved indication).

items.name: Lean Mass Support
items.description: Enhanced GH promotes protein synthesis and lean tissue preservation.

items.name: Sleep Quality
items.description: GH secretagogues often improve deep sleep quality.

items.name: Recovery Enhancement
items.description: Enhanced GH supports tissue repair and recovery from exercise.

items.name: Anti-Aging Support
items.description: Restoring more youthful GH levels may provide anti-aging benefits.

items.name: Lipid Profile
items.description: Tesamorelin has shown improvements in lipid parameters in studies.

items.name: IGF-1 Optimization
items.description: Increased GH leads to increased IGF-1 production.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection, typically administered before bed or in the morning on an empty stomach. Evening dosing may enhance natural nighttime GH pulse. Avoid eating 2-3 hours before and 30-60 minutes after injection for optimal results.

protocols.notes: Inject on empty stomach; avoid food 30-60 min after
Higher Tesamorelin for fat loss focus
Split dosing for sustained GH support

steps: Clean vial top with alcohol pad
Add 2mL bacteriostatic water for convenient dosing

keyBenefits: Evening dosing enhances natural GH pulse
Flexible once or twice daily options

interactions.notes: Already contains Tesamorelin - do not add more.
Already contains Ipamorelin - do not add more.
Both Tesamorelin and CJC-1295 are GHRH analogs; combining may cause excessive stimulation.
Already has GHRP pathway covered by Ipamorelin; adding more GHRPs may be excessive.
Both affect GH release; combination may be excessive and affect glucose.
Different mechanisms; can complement for healing with GH support.
Different pathways; some combine for body composition goals.
GH affects glucose metabolism; diabetics should monitor carefully.

timeline.effects: Improved sleep quality often noticed first; initial GH response
Enhanced recovery; subtle body composition changes beginning
Noticeable fat loss (especially visceral); improved energy
Full body composition benefits; continued improvements

contraindications: Active malignancy (GH may promote tumor growth)

good: Certificate of Analysis with ratios verified
warning: Tesamorelin is FDA-approved as Egrifta - research blends are not

references.title: Metabolic effects of a growth hormone-releasing factor in patients with HIV
references.authors: Falutz J, Allas S, Blot K, et al.
references.keyFindings: Landmark RCT in 412 HIV patients: tesamorelin 2 mg daily for 26 weeks reduced visceral fat by 10.9% vs 0.6% placebo, improved lipid profiles with no change in subcutaneous fat.

references.title: Ipamorelin, the first selective growth hormone secretagogue
references.authors: Raun K, Hansen BS, Johansen NL, et al.
references.keyFindings: Ipamorelin is the first GHRP-receptor agonist with selectivity for GH release similar to GHRH; does not release ACTH or cortisol even at doses over 200-fold the ED50 for GH.

references.title: GHRH + GHRP Synergy
references.keyFindings: Combining GHRH and GHRP pathways produces synergistic GH release greater than either agent alone, driven by independent receptor mechanisms on pituitary somatotrophs.

references.title: No Published Studies on Tesa/IPA Combination
references.keyFindings: No clinical trials have evaluated the specific tesamorelin + ipamorelin blend; synergy is inferred from GHRH/GHRP pharmacology and individual component data.

latestResearch.title: Efficacy and safety of tesamorelin in people with HIV on integrase inhibitors
latestResearch.summary: First dedicated study in patients on INSTI-based regimens: tesamorelin significantly reduced visceral fat, hepatic fat, and trunk-to-appendicular fat ratio with no worsening of glycemic control.

latestResearch.title: Effects of Tesamorelin on Neurocognitive Impairment in Persons With HIV and Abdominal Obesity
latestResearch.summary: Phase 2 RCT in 73 participants showed tesamorelin reduced waist circumference vs standard of care, though neurocognitive benefits did not reach statistical significance.

latestResearch.title: Body composition, hepatic fat, metabolic, and safety outcomes of Tesamorelin in HIV-associated lipodystrophy: A meta-analysis
latestResearch.summary: Meta-analysis of five RCTs confirmed tesamorelin significantly reduces visceral adipose tissue in adults with HIV-associated lipodystrophy.

quickStats.frequency: Once daily (evening preferred) or twice daily for advanced protocols
